summary: Wave 2A Phase-1 trace-batch screening completed with perfect effective baseline agreement after replacing Cardinal c0811 node failures with reruns excluding that node; proceed to Wave 2B with legacy Phase-1 default.

dirty_files: untracked generated Wave 0/1/2A scenario and fixture files

runs.notes: Original Cardinal run had 5 trace failures, all on c0811.
Failed-only Cardinal rerun used --exclude=c0811 and replaced original failed Cardinal scenarios.

decision: Keep phase1_trace_batch_policy=legacy for Wave 2B; retain cap16 as optional conservative resource candidate for later interaction testing, and cap1 as diagnostic only.
infrastructure_note: Cardinal node c0811 produced CUDA misaligned address, illegal memory access, launch failure, and CUBLAS internal errors during Phase-0/model-forward traces; reruns on c0802/c0804/c0822/c0826 succeeded, so avoid or report c0811 for exact-trace work.

summary: Wave 2B Phase-4 scheduler/refresh/ranker/executor screening completed; deferred_v1 is the primary Phase-4 candidate, streaming_v1 is secondary, planner_v2 is rejected for compact graph drift.

dirty_files: untracked generated Wave 0/1/2A/2B scenario and fixture files

run.notes: Cardinal jobs were submitted with --exclude=c0811 after Wave 2A node failures.

decision: Promote phase4_refresh_policy=deferred_v1 as the primary Wave 2B candidate and phase4_row_executor=streaming_v1 as a secondary interaction candidate; reject planner_v2 and refresh_opt_v1; proceed to Wave 2C as an independent row/encoder/staging family screen using legacy Phase-1 and legacy Phase-4 defaults.

summary: Wave 2C row/encoder/staging/planner screening completed with exact compact graph agreement for every variant; row_subchunk_512 and feature_batch_planner are promoted for Wave 3 consideration.

dirty_files: untracked generated Wave 0/1/2A/2B/2C scenario and fixture files

run.notes: Cardinal jobs were submitted with --exclude=c0811 after Wave 2A node failures.

decision: Promote row_subchunk_size=512 as the primary row/memory candidate and plan_feature_batch_size=true as the conservative memory candidate for Wave 3 interactions; do not promote row_fadvise, active CPU/pinned CPU encoder residency, or no_cpu_staging as ordinary candidates.

summary: Wave 2 advanced-family screening is complete; Wave 3 should combine only locked stable resources plus selected Phase-4 and row/memory candidates.

infrastructure_note: Continue excluding Cardinal c0811 unless explicitly diagnosing that node.

run.run_goal: Confirm whether selected Wave 2 candidates combine safely without compact-graph drift and identify the best runtime/memory candidate for promotion.

summary: Wave 3 interaction-confirmation sweep completed successfully. All 42 scenarios matched Wave 0 fp32 baselines exactly; singleton row_subchunk_512 had the best aggregate runtime signal, while combined interactions and streaming did not improve enough for global promotion.

dirty_files: untracked generated Wave 0/1/2/3 scenario and fixture files

cluster_tier_notes.ascend: No singleton was a clear Ascend-wide speed win; row_subchunk_512 helped anomaly but slowed fast prompts.
cluster_tier_notes.cardinal: deferred_v1, row_subchunk_512, and plan_feature_batch_size all improved Cardinal runtime ratios, with deferred_v1 strongest overall on Cardinal.

decisions.promote_primary: row_subchunk_size=512 as the primary broader-test/default candidate

summary: Launched full-answer trajectory generation for canonical 828_base GSM prompt on Ascend to estimate generated-token count and downstream trace sharding needs.
next_step: When trajectory.json exists, inspect generated token count/text and decide selected-token policy plus shard count for trace batching.

summary: Canonical 828_base full-answer trajectory generation completed, but hit the 256-token cap before final answer/stop; generated-token count and candidate trace selections were inspected for batching estimates.

trajectory.generated_text_prefix: Here's how to solve the problem step-by-step:

interpretation: Generation plumbing is fine; this prompt needs a higher max_new_tokens for a complete final answer. For a first real trace batch, lean_every32_surprisal8 is the safer launch; numeric_every32 is broader but about 3x the selected-prefix-token cost. All-token tracing should wait until selected-token runs calibrate per-token runtime.
next_step: Either rerun trajectory with a larger cap for a complete answer, or launch the 15-token lean selected trace plan with 4 shards and conservative walltime to calibrate runtime.

summary: Relaunched canonical 828_base full-answer trajectory with max_new_tokens=1024 after the 256-token run hit the cap before final answer; goal is complete response then all-token trace packing for 2h target jobs/3h walltime.

packing_goal.packing_basis: after trajectory completes, estimate cost per token as prompt_token_count + generated_index and choose shard_count from observed output length plus previous trace timings

next_step: When trajectory.json exists, inspect whether it stopped naturally and build all-token trace specs/shards sized for ~2h target runtime.

summary: Canonical 828_base cap1024 trajectory completed with a natural stop and final answer; built all-token trace specs for 339 generated tokens and packed candidate all-token trace shards for 2h target/3h walltime planning.

packing_estimate.calibration_primary: prior Wave4 828_base single next-token duration 773.15s at prefix length 73 => 10.59s per prefix-token
packing_estimate.alternate_linear_model: fit through cheap smoke prefix23=450.39s and Wave4 prefix73=773.15s gives ~175.5 total GPU-hours and 96 shards below 2h, but primary model is more conservative for 828.
packing_estimate.note: The cheap 2+2 smoke prefix-rate model is much slower and likely over-conservative because it used a different prompt/activation profile; if it were used literally, many more jobs would be needed.

recommendation: Launch all-token trace with shards_144.json and --walltime 03:00:00; this uses 144 array tasks, each with 2-3 generated-token traces, targeting about 2h under the 828 prefix-rate model.

summary: Revised all-token 828 packing from fixed-count LPT balancing to target-cost contiguous packing, so early short-prefix traces are grouped heavily while late long-prefix traces are mostly singletons/pairs.

calibration.primary: Wave4 828_base duration 773.15s at prefix 73 => 10.59s per prefix-token

decision: Prefer cap680 contiguous shards for first all-token launch: 166 array tasks, early shards pack up to 8 traces, late shards mostly one or two traces, with 03:00:00 walltime.

summary: Submitted first partial all-token full-answer trace batch for 828_base on Ascend quad: LPT shards 0-39 of 144, 40 array tasks, 3h walltime.

next_step: Monitor array 5404207; aggregate after shard subset completes and inspect trace timings/errors before submitting remaining shard ranges.

next_step: Monitor original job 5404207 and rerun job 5404392; aggregate after all intended shard ids 0-39 have successful trace_results rows.

summary: First 40 LPT shards of the 828_base all-token full-answer trace completed successfully after rerunning a0001 failures; aggregation found 120/120 token traces ok with compact graphs saved.

projection.note: Observed runtime is close to the 2h target and below 3h walltime; one shard reached 2.08h elapsed but completed safely.

recommendation: Use the same LPT shards_144 packing for remaining shards, continue excluding a0001, and submit remaining shards in 40-ish task waves (40-79, 80-119, 120-143) or one larger wave if queue pressure is acceptable.

summary: Submitted remaining 828_base all-token full-answer trace shards on Ascend quad in three waves, all excluding a0001 after earlier CUDA-device failures.
next_step: Monitor jobs 5408376, 5408377, and 5408378. When complete, aggregate the shared run_root and verify 339 ok token traces across 144 shards.

summary: Canonical 828_base full-answer all-token trace completed: every generated token from the 339-token trajectory traced successfully across 144 Ascend quad shards, with compact graphs saved and final aggregation clean.
interpretation: The Track-2 full-answer harness now has one complete all-generated-token exact-trace trajectory for 828_base. Later shard waves finished faster than the first 40-shard projection because the LPT shard ids submitted later had lower observed walltimes; future launches can use these final timing summaries for better packing/wave sizing.
next_step: Use this run as the first graph-evolution dataset, then prepare analogous full-answer trajectories for 361_base and selected correct/incorrect or high-temperature variants before committing more all-token trace GPU-hours.

summary: Generated first and richer temporal graph-evolution diagnostics for the completed 828_base full-answer all-token run; no monotonic rising-to-answer story is obvious, but positionless feature reuse and layer-flow aggregation show substantially more stability than exact edge identity.

trajectory_correctness.note: The generated reasoning briefly detoured through an erroneous 40-80=-40 calculation, then re-read/self-corrected to 140-80=60.

interpretation: Exact feature-position and edge identity churn substantially over the trajectory. Ignoring position reveals stronger feature reuse, top/mass-core edge views are more stable than full edge identity, and layer-level source-to-logit flow is highly stable. The correct trajectory does not show a simple global monotonic stabilization toward the final answer; future comparisons should test whether these aggregate stability signals distinguish self-correction/correctness across multiple trajectories.
